note: This artifact classifies proof/refinement strength. It does not grant coverage credit; current per-invariant lane status and check_id credit remain receipt-derived in docs/invariants/coverage-matrix.json.

entries.title: Receipt-bound coverage authority
entries.claim: A coverage cell is authoritative only when fresh generated artifacts, tool-run receipts, stale-check and the derived coverage report agree.
entries.scope: Per-lane status, check_id credit and covered/not_applicable cells for active invariants.
entries.limits: This scope artifact describes evidence strength only; it does not create or upgrade coverage.

entries.title: Replay concrete oracle
entries.claim: Replay checks concrete bundle-bound traces and refutes invalid scenarios with stable error codes.
entries.scope: Concrete trace semantics, payload binding, authz freshness/policy checks and executable negative controls.
entries.limits: Replay is not an exhaustive interleaving or abstract proof of every possible runtime execution.
entries.verification: scripts/check-verification-full.sh replay prerequisite and replay negative-control blocks

entries.title: Alloy structural models
entries.claim: Alloy gives small-scope structural counterexample search over generated bundle/scenario facts.
entries.authority_surface: verification/formal-full/alloy/core plus verification/formal-full/alloy/generated and Alloy tool-run receipts
entries.scope: Structural lifecycle, anchor, witness, lease, authz and drain relations where the generated model has facts and assertions.
entries.limits: Alloy cells are not evidence for time/freshness, overlay, routing or constraint-update behavior unless the generated model explicitly carries that obligation.
entries.verification: AlloyRunner blocks and Alloy negative controls inside scripts/check-verification-full.sh

entries.title: P generated monitors
entries.claim: P checks generated protocol monitors and bounded interleaving controls for modeled event streams.
entries.scope: Event-order monitors, payload-grounding monitors, drain/race controls and planned interleaving hooks that are explicitly run by the gate.
entries.limits: P does not prove Rust implementation paths or lanes not represented by generated monitors.
entries.verification: P protocol run and P monitor-firing negative controls inside scripts/check-verification-full.sh

entries.title: Kani bounded Rust rules
entries.claim: Kani checks Rust-facing pure reducers and validators over bounded nondeterministic input spaces configured by the lane profile.
entries.authority_surface: verification/formal-full/kani/profile.json plus generated Kani harnesses and tool-run receipts
entries.scope: Bounded executable confirmation that selected Rust predicates match intended rules for active coverage cells.
entries.limits: Kani is not an unbounded proof and does not cover external IO or adapter behavior outside the harness shape.
entries.verification: Kani lane in scripts/check-verification-full.sh and schema validation of verification/formal-full/kani/profile.json

entries.title: Verus code-adjacent proofs
entries.claim: Verus proves preservation and rule lemmas over generated Rust-like proof artifacts under the always-on no-cheating profile.
entries.scope: Pure kernel/rule preservation lemmas that are generated from Formal IR and credited by the coverage report.
entries.limits: Verus does not by itself prove external adapters, concrete storage, network IO or all parser behavior.

entries.title: Lean4 abstract theorem applications
entries.claim: Lean4 proves generated scenario-bound theorem applications over the abstract protocol vocabulary and generated facts.
entries.authority_surface: verification/formal-full/lean core package, verification/formal-full/lean4/generated and Lean4 receipts
entries.scope: Abstract protocol theorem applications connected to generated Formal IR payloads and credited by the coverage report.
entries.limits: Lean4 alone does not claim Rust implementation correctness; that connection comes from replay, Kani and Verus evidence.
entries.verification: lake build plus lake env lean inside scripts/check-verification-full.sh

entries.title: Cryptographic and toolchain assumptions
entries.claim: Hash collision resistance, signature primitive correctness and external solver/compiler correctness are trusted assumptions, not repository proofs.
entries.authority_surface: toolchain pins, HMAC/SHA tests and formal-doctor installation checks
entries.scope: Environmental and cryptographic assumptions needed for the evidence chain to be meaningful.
entries.limits: The repository tests deterministic use of primitives but does not prove cryptographic hardness or solver soundness.
entries.verification: tools/formal-doctor, tools/formal-install, unit tests and pinned tool versions

entries.title: External provider CI adoption
entries.claim: Provider-specific PR CI wiring is outside the repository proof contour until a workflow supplies the baseline and changed-file source.
entries.scope: Local and repo-gate enforcement exists; external provider adoption remains integration work.
entries.limits: No checked-in provider workflow is claimed by this milestone.

entries.title: Future invariants and unbounded IO
entries.claim: Planned invariants, deeper refinement theorems, unbounded parser/IO behavior and external adapter semantics are future obligations until generated artifacts and receipts exist.
entries.authority_surface: formal catalogs, obligation manifest and product-track future milestones
entries.scope: Target-state formal surface that is documented but not current coverage.
entries.limits: Catalog mentions are not coverage and must remain out of current proof claims until the coverage matrix credits them.
entries.verification: formal-discipline-check, coverage-matrix drift check and generated proof/refinement scope check
